title: PreviewConfig schema definition in packages/schema
description: Finalize the `PreviewConfig` type (placeholder created in TASK-3) describing what a team's `*.preview.tsx` file exports: the component reference, its variants, and interactive controls.
acceptanceCriteria: `PreviewConfig` type has `component: string` (display name), `variants: PreviewVariant[]`, `controls: PreviewControl[]`
`PreviewVariant` has `id: string`, `label: string`, `props: Record<string, unknown>` (initial prop values for that variant)
`PreviewControl` supports at least `text`, `boolean`, and `select` control kinds, each with `propName: string` mapping to a prop the control mutates at runtime
Type is exported from `packages/schema`'s barrel and replaces the TASK-3 placeholder without breaking existing imports
A JSDoc comment on `PreviewConfig` documents the expected shape of a `*.preview.tsx` file's default export for connector/preview-kit implementers

steps.details: In `packages/schema/src/preview-config.ts`, replace the placeholder with `interface PreviewVariant { id: string; label: string; props: Record<string, unknown> }` and a discriminated union `PreviewControl` for `text | boolean | select` kinds, each with `propName` and kind-specific fields (e.g. `select` has `options: string[]`).
`interface PreviewConfig { component: string; variants: PreviewVariant[]; controls: PreviewControl[] }`.

Add a JSDoc block above `PreviewConfig` showing an example: `export default { component: 'Button', variants: [...], controls: [...] } satisfies PreviewConfig` alongside the actual React component export expected by the harness.
Run `tsc --noEmit` across the workspace to confirm the finalized type doesn't break the TASK-29 placeholder usage or any other existing import of `PreviewConfig`.

technicalNotes: This type is the contract between three independently-built pieces: the connector (TASK-39/40/41, reads `*.preview.tsx`), preview-kit (TASK-38, renders it), and the admin panel's controls UI (TASK-49, drives it via postMessage) â€” keep it stable once teams start writing real `*.preview.tsx` files against it.
